_comment: WIN-SYS-006 macro manager / organizer / IDE & security prompts. A three-part fail-closed contract, all py-static over comment-stripped source + XML + the shared CSV; no native build, pixels, or runtime is claimed (runtime_verified stays false). PART 1 destructive_conversions: the single shared basctl QueryDel() funnel -- through which every Basic-IDE macro/dialog/library/module delete-or-overwrite confirmation flows -- is converted off its raw VclMessageType::Question / VclButtonsType::YesNo box onto the shared sfx2::ConfirmDestructiveAction helper, with the destructive verb passed PER CALLER ('Overwrite' for QueryReplaceMacro, 'Delete' for the four deletions) and each RID_STR_QUERYDEL* primary wording preserved (including the QueryDelLibRef 'reference to' distinction). The migration is registered HERE, not in dialog-anatomy-policy.json, so it does not consume WIN-DLG-001's migration budget. PART 2 security_prompt: the macro-EXECUTION prompt (uui macrowarnmedium.ui + secmacrowarnings.cxx) is pinned read-only so the SAFE action 'Disable Macros' (cancel, RET_CANCEL) stays both the GTK default (has-default) and the initially-focused control (mxDisableBtn->grab_focus()), while 'Enable Macros' (ok, RET_OK) is never wired as default or focus -- so an accidental Enter can never run untrusted macros. PART 3 modal_surface_ledger: a read-only subset assertion that every row-owned macro/organizer/security dialog root stays policy=native-exclusion in the shared dialog-notification-policy.csv (fails closed on any drop or reclassification to a bottom-right notification form). The ledger adds no .ui and therefore triggers no CSV regeneration. The macro-security-LEVEL / certificate / signature dialogs (WIN-SYS-007) and the custom-drawn Basic IDE code editor are deliberately out of scope.

markers.note: the exported helper entry point basctl links against (SFX2_DLLPUBLIC, weld parent)
the per-caller verb-named destructive label field

present_markers.note: the shared helper header is included
QueryDel builds the shared destructive-confirmation params
the shared consequence line is wired as the secondary text
the destructive button label is the per-caller verb parameter, not hard-coded
QueryDel dispatches through the shared helper

absent_markers.note: the raw Yes/No destructive box (Yes=Enter-answerable destructive) is gone
the raw Question/YesNo QueryDel box is gone

call_sites.pattern: QueryDel( rName, IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELMACRO ), IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELBTN ), pParent )
QueryDel( rName, IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYREPLACEMACRO ), IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYREPLACEBTN ), pParent )
QueryDel( rName, IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELDIALOG ), IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELBTN ), pParent )
QueryDel( rName, IDEResId( bRef ? RID_STR_QUERYDELLIBREF : RID_STR_QUERYDELLIB ), IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELBTN ), pParent )
QueryDel( rName, IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELMODULE ), IDEResId( RID_STR_QUERYDELBTN ), pParent )

preserved_strings.pattern: RID_STR_QUERYDELMACRO", "Do you want to delete the macro XX?
RID_STR_QUERYREPLACEMACRO", "Do you want to overwrite the XX macro?
RID_STR_QUERYDELDIALOG", "Do you want to delete the XX dialog?
RID_STR_QUERYDELLIB", "Do you want to delete the XX library?
RID_STR_QUERYDELLIBREF", "Do you want to delete the reference to the XX library?
preserved_strings.note: the library-REFERENCE distinction is preserved (must not collapse into plain delete-library)

preserved_strings.pattern: RID_STR_QUERYDELMODULE", "Do you want to delete the XX module?

safe_button.note: 'Disable Macros' -- the safe action, the GTK default

source_present_markers.note: the safe Disable button takes the initial focus

source_absent_markers.note: the unsafe Enable button must never grab the initial focus

modal_surface_ledger.note: Read-only subset assertion over the shared dialog-notification-policy.csv. Every row-owned macro-manager / organizer / IDE / security dialog root must stay native-exclusion (kept modal by the NotificationRouter); a drop or a flip to a bottom-right notification form fails closed. This adds no .ui and does not regenerate the CSV.
